pr.title: Misc matches from src-only build work
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Cherry-picked a set of source-only-build-driven hot-path matches needed for loading the game and rendering early screens. The PR made gm_1A36.c matching, added several Dolphin SDK math/SI matches, filled small game-mode/library functions, corrected related types and headers, and renamed the controller BSS layout from separate gm_80479C30/controller_union symbols into a single controller_map object. decomp.dev reported 14 new matches, +3724 matched-code bytes, and +2248 linked-code bytes.
what_changed: Marked melee/gm/gm_1A36.c as Matching in configure.py after completing the remaining controller-map functions.
Reworked the gm_1A36 controller BSS model: symbols.txt replaced gm_80479C30 plus local controller_union with one global controller_map object of size 0x100 at 0x80479C30.

Changed gm_1A36.static.h from a four-entry gm_controller_map plus separate union and globals into a static struct controller_map containing x0[5], callback xF0, and repeat-delay/timing fields xF4 through xFE.

Matched gm_1A36 functions including gm_801A3A74, gm_801A3820, fn_801A396C, and gm_801A3E88; the code now copies HSD_PadCopyStatus, synthesizes high 32-bit button aliases, maintains an aggregate controller slot at index 4, and initializes controller_map with default repeat timings and fn_801A396C.
Made gm_801A3820 and fn_801A396C static implementation details and removed their public declarations from gm_1A36.h.
Cleaned gm_1A36.h include guards to MELEE_GM_1A36_H and reduced broad includes; added gm_801A3EF4 declaration and moved dependent include sites toward gm_unsplit.h where needed.
Implemented Dolphin SDK matches PSMTXRotAxisRad, C_MTXLookAt, and PSMTXMultVecSR, including paired-single inline assembly guarded with __MWERKS__.

Improved OSSerial matches: __SITransfer now preserves SI_COMCSR register state before setting fields, SIGetStatus takes a channel argument and extracts per-channel status under disabled interrupts, and SIEnablePolling removes a direct zero write to __SIRegs[0x30/4].
Updated OSSerial.h signature from SIGetStatus(void) to SIGetStatus(int).
Implemented gm_80164600 as an all-unlockable-stages check using NUM_UNLOCKABLE_STAGES and a u16 stage-unlock bitmask from gmMainLib_8015EDA4.
Adjusted gmMainLib_8015ED8C and gmMainLib_8015EDA4 to return typed fields inside thing.x1868/x186A instead of M2C_FIELD/manual casts; gmMainLib_8015EDA4 now returns u16* instead of s16*.
Split struct gmm_x1868 into explicit u16 x1868 unlocked-character bitmask and u16 x186A unlocked-stage bitmask.
Implemented gm_16F1 fn_80172C78 as a bool-returning helper that checks gmMainLib_8015D984, calls gmMainLib_8015DA68 on success, and returns true/false; header return type changed from void to bool.
Implemented lb_8000FCDC to allocate two buffers via HSD_MemAlloc and then call lb_8000FA94.
Reworked lbrefract acosf to use __frsqrte/Newton refinement for 1.0f - x*x and explicit NAN/INF edge handling; also corrected an assert file string from refract.c to lbrefract.c.

Changed several files including dbitem.c, gm_1AED.c, gm_1BF9.c, vi0401.c, vi0402.c, and vi0601.c to include gm_unsplit.h instead of direct gm_1A36.h and nearby gm headers; gm_unsplit.h gained gm_1A4C.h and gm_1ADD.h.
smart_moves: Used src-only build work to identify practical hot-path functions first, matching code needed for boot, game load, and title-screen rendering rather than isolated low-impact routines.
Recognized that controller_union was not truly separate from gm_80479C30 but the fifth entry of a larger controller_map array/object, resolving both BSS symbols and C access patterns.
Kept gm_1A36 helper routines static once their real linkage was understood, reducing header pollution and improving match fidelity.
Modeled saved-data bitmasks as explicit u16 fields x1868 and x186A, which allowed game-mode unlock helpers to become simple typed accesses instead of offset macros.
Used MWERKS inline assembly and clang-format guards for paired-single SDK routines where C alone would not plausibly match generated code.
Added PAD_STACK(0x10) and dont_inline pragmas where needed for gm_1A36/gm_16F1 matching rather than overcomplicating source logic.
Updated symbols.txt, headers, and configure.py together, keeping object matching status, global BSS naming, and public prototypes consistent.
decomp_lessons: When adjacent BSS symbols look like an array plus a union, check whether callers index one past the nominal array; in this PR the aggregate controller slot was represented as controller_map.x0[4].
A match can depend on retyping storage, not just rewriting functions: gmMainLib_8015EDA4 only became clean after struct gmm_x1868 exposed the unlocked-stage u16 at x186A.
For split gm modules with circular or broad dependencies, gm_unsplit.h can be a pragmatic include target while individual headers are narrowed.
Public headers should not expose local/static helpers just because the function names are known; remove declarations when linkage evidence shows file-local functions.
SDK functions that use paired-single instructions may need direct MWERKS asm blocks; preserve register reuse and odd source patterns when they are part of the match.
For status-register SDK code, preserving existing register bits before setting bitfields can be the key difference between a near-match and a full match.
Use named constants such as MJ_COUNT and NUM_UNLOCKABLE_STAGES when they document known sentinel/count semantics without hurting the match.
naming_conventions: BSS object renamed from gm_80479C30/controller_union to controller_map once the layout was understood.
Controller-entry struct kept as struct gm_controller_map with offset comments for button, trigger, repeat, release, unk, x28, and x2C.
Wrapper struct named struct controller_map with field names x0, xF0, xF4, xF6, xF8, xFA, xFC, and xFE because only offsets/roles were partially understood.
Header guard changed from address-style GALE01_1A3680 to project-style MELEE_GM_1A36_H.
Saved-data bitmask fields were named x1868 and x186A with comments identifying unlocked characters and unlocked stages.
Used C_ prefix for C SDK variants such as C_MTXLookAt and PS prefix for paired-single variants such as PSMTXRotAxisRad and PSMTXMultVecSR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PSMTXRotAxisRad uses register variables, reuses the rad register for loaded vector data, and emits MWERKS paired-single asm with psq_l, ps_merge00, ps_muls0, ps_muls1, ps_neg, ps_sum0, ps_sum1, and psq_st.
PSMTXMultVecSR is implemented as an asm function using paired-single loads, multiply-adds, ps_sum0 reductions, and single-lane stores to Vec.x/y/z.
gm_801A3A74 uses PAD_STACK(0x10), u8 casts on HSD_PadCopyStatus indexing, and explicit (u64)1 << 32..39 expressions to match high-bit controller aliases.
fn_80172C78 is wrapped with #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop to preserve expected call structure.
acosf matching uses __frsqrte and repeated Newton-Raphson refinement instead of a simpler helper expression.
SIGetStatus disables/restores interrupts around SI status extraction and shifts by ((3 - arg0) * 8), matching per-channel status behavior.
C_MTXLookAt preserves expression ordering in dot-product negations, which can matter for floating-point codegen.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported 14 new matches and quantified the improvement as matched code 36.52% (+0.10%, +3724 bytes) and linked code 24.64% (+0.06%, +2248 bytes).

The bot listed new full matches for gm_801A3A74, C_MTXLookAt, gm_801A3820, PSMTXRotAxisRad, fn_801A396C, acosf, gm_80164600, SIGetStatus, gm_801A3E88, SIEnablePolling, PSMTXMultVecSR, fn_80172C78, lb_8000FCDC, and __SITransfer.
searchable_terms: Misc matches from src-only build work
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type struct controller_map fields xF4 through xFE.
Look for follow-up work replacing gm_unsplit.h includes with narrower gm headers after dependency cleanup.
Search for additional Dolphin mtx paired-single routines matched using the PSMTXRotAxisRad or PSMTXMultVecSR patterns.
Check whether TypeTime in OSSerial.c is later used or typed more accurately.
Search for future saved-data struct refinements around gmm_x1868, unlocked characters, and unlocked stages.
